name: Yamini Week3 - CodeBasics AI Support Agent

nodes.name: Prepare Input
nodes.notes: Captures the learner query + metadata to log. Edit learnerName / learnerEmail or wire them from your real intake form/webhook.

nodes.name: Download FAQ from Drive
nodes.notes: Pick Yamini_Week3_FAQ_Knowledge_Base.xlsx from the list after import. Requires a Google Drive credential.

nodes.name: Google Gemini Chat Model
nodes.notes: Requires a 'Google Gemini (PaLM) API' credential - get a free API key at https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey. Any chat model works; swap the provider if you prefer.

parameters.jsonSchemaExample:
{
  "category": "course_access",
  "intent": "Learner cannot log in to access their purchased course",
  "needs_human": false,
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "answer": "Hi! Sorry you're having trouble logging in. Please try the 'Forgot Password' link on the login page to reset your password, and make sure you're using the same email you purchased with. If it still doesn't work, reply here and we'll dig deeper."
}

parameters.text:
=You are the AI Support Agent for CodeBasics, an online ed-tech platform that sells self-paced data/AI/coding courses and runs live cohorts.

Answer the learner's question using ONLY the FAQ knowledge base below. Be friendly, concise and helpful.

=== FAQ KNOWLEDGE BASE ===
{{ $json.faqText }}
=== END FAQ ===

LEARNER QUERY:
"{{ $json.query }}"

Classify the query into one of:
course_access, certificate, refund, discount_sale, course_guidance, payment_issue, other.

Set needs_human = true ONLY for genuine edge cases that the FAQ cannot safely resolve:
- Billing disputes, or "payment failed but money was deducted / not refunded"
- Real technical bugs the FAQ does not cover
- Partnership / legal / account-specific issues not covered by the FAQ
- Any query where the FAQ has no confident answer (confidence < 0.5)

For everything else (login/course access, certificate download, standard refund-policy questions, discounts/sales, course guidance), set needs_human = false and write the answer directly from the FAQ.

If needs_human is true, still write a short, polite holding message in the 'answer' field telling the learner their request is being escalated to a human.

assignments.name: response
assignments.value: =Hi {{ $('Prepare Input').item.json.learnerName }}, thanks for reaching out. This one needs a specialist, so I've escalated it to our human support team. Someone will follow up with you at {{ $('Prepare Input').item.json.learnerEmail }} shortly. Reference: {{ $('Prepare Input').item.json.sessionId }}.

parameters.subject: =[Escalation] {{ $('Classify & Draft Answer').item.json.output.category }} - learner support

nodes.name: Notify Support Team
nodes.notes: Optional: emails the human support queue. Needs a Gmail credential. Delete this node if you don't want the email step - the sheet row (Status = Escalated) already acts as the human queue.

nodes.name: Log to Yamini_week3_Responses
nodes.notes: Set documentId to your Yamini_week3_Responses sheet (paste URL or pick from list). Add a header row with: Timestamp | Session ID | Learner Name | Learner Email | Query | Category | Intent | Needs Human | Confidence | Status | Response. Requires a Google Sheets credential.

nodes.name: Format Reply
nodes.notes: The 'output' field is what the chat window shows back to the learner.
